source_pr.title: executor: fix data race in the temp folder
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65063

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

These test cases read the same temp folder concurrently. so it will lead to the flaky test.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Executor spill-related tests were flaky because multiple tests shared the same temp-storage folder: concurrent runs could create/read/delete files in the same directory, making file-leak checks nondeterministic.
failure_signature: executor spill tests: intermittent failures in temp-file leak checks due to shared temp-storage path collisions across concurrent tests
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness came from shared filesystem state: several spill tests used the same global temp-storage path and walked that directory to assert “no leaked files”. When tests run in parallel, they can observe each other’s temporary files (or race with cleanup), causing intermittent failures. This PR fixes it by isolating `TempStoragePath` per test using `t.TempDir()` (with proper restore) and by making the leak-check walk the per-test directory instead of a shared global path.
fix_pattern: Avoid shared temp-storage paths in tests; set `TempStoragePath` to a per-test `t.TempDir()` (restore afterward) and scope leak checks to that directory to prevent cross-test interference.

evidence: evidence: These test cases read the same temp folder concurrently. so it will lead to the flaky test.

files: pkg/executor/aggregate/BUILD.bazel, pkg/executor/aggregate/agg_spill_test.go, pkg/executor/internal/util/util.go, pkg/executor/join/BUILD.bazel, pkg/executor/join/inner_join_spill_test.go, pkg/executor/join/left_outer_semi_join_probe_test.go, pkg/executor/join/outer_join_spill_test.go, pkg/executor/join/semi_join_probe_test.go, pkg/executor/sortexec/parallel_sort_spill_test.go, pkg/executor/sortexec/sort_spill_test.go, pkg/executor/sortexec/sortexec_pkg_test.go, pkg/executor/sortexec/topn_spill_test.go
